reasoning: We have a 31-day window (March 1 to April 1, 2026) for an official election announcement. Recent events show Nicolás Maduro was captured by the US in early January 2026, and Vice President Delcy Rodríguez took over as interim president. Although the Venezuelan constitution typically mandates elections within 30 days of the president's absence, this deadline has already passed (early February) with no announcement. Key actors on both sides explicitly oppose an immediate election timeline. US President Donald Trump stated that a 30-day timeline is unrealistic and the country needs to be 'fixed' first. Similarly, National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez stated on February 10, 2026, that elections will not be held in the "immediate future," and Delcy Rodríguez's mid-February interviews confirm she is slow-walking political reforms without providing any date. Since the legal authorities (CNE and interim government) are firmly against announcing a date and their primary external pressure (the US) also favors a delay, an untelegraphed announcement in the next month is highly improbable.
